checks.border: The board should have a black border all the way around the edge.
checks.topLeftWhite: The top-left square should be white (it's a light square).
checks.rightOfTopLeftDark: The square to the right of the top-left one should be dark brown (a dark square).
checks.bottomLeftDark: The bottom-left square should be dark brown (a dark square).
checks.bottomRightWhite: The bottom-right square should be white.
checks.charcoalPiece: There should be a charcoal piece on the dark square in the top row.
checks.topRim: The pieces in the top rows should have a rim around them.
checks.whitePiece: There should be a white piece on the dark square in the bottom row.
checks.bottomRim: The pieces in the bottom rows should have a rim around them.
checks.rimRatio: The rim of a piece should be 80% as wide as its square - check the outer circle's radius.
checks.innerRatio: The centre of a piece should be 75% as wide as the whole piece - check the inner circle's radius.
checks.noPieceOnLight: There shouldn't be a piece on the top-left square - pieces only go on the dark squares.
checks.middleRowsEmpty: The middle rows should be empty - only the top and bottom rows have pieces.

setUpTheBoard.name: Set up the board
setUpTheBoard.description: Draw the checkerboard and place the pieces on the dark squares of the top and bottom rows - and make it work for any board size.

board8.name: Set up an 8×8 board
board8.description: Draw the classic 8×8 checkerboard and lay out the pieces ready to play.

board6.name: Set up a 6×6 board
board6.description: The same code should scale down to a smaller 6×6 board.

board10.name: Set up a 10×10 board
board10.description: And it should scale up to a larger 10×10 board.

wholeGrid.question: How do I draw a whole grid of squares rather than just one?
wholeGrid.answer: Use nested loops: an outer loop that steps through the rows and an inner loop that steps through the columns. Draw one square each time the inner loop runs.

anyBoardSize.question: How do I make it work whatever the board size is?
anyBoardSize.answer: Don't pick a fixed square size. Ask `getBoardSize()` how many squares there are, work out a single `cell` size from that and the space available, and build every position as a multiple of it.

alternateColours.question: How do I make the squares alternate colours?
alternateColours.answer: Look at a square's row and column together. The remainder operator (`%`) lets you split every square into one of two groups based on `row` and `col`, and those two groups are exactly the light and dark squares.

whichSquaresGetPieces.question: Which squares get pieces?
whichSquaresGetPieces.answer: Pieces only sit on the **dark** squares, so you can reuse the same test you used to colour them. Then narrow it further to only the top and bottom rows, leaving the middle two empty.

ridgedPiece.question: How do I draw a ridged piece?
ridgedPiece.answer: Draw two circles at the same centre: a larger one for the rim, then a slightly smaller one on top in the piece's colour. Base both radii on the cell size so the pieces scale with the board.

rimSize.question: The size of the rim doesn't look like the sample diagram.
rimSize.answer: Check that the inner circle is 75% of the outer circle (not 75% of the square).

rectangle.description: Draw a rectangle at position (left, top) with the given width, height, and color

circle.description: Draw a circle centered at (centerX, centerY) with the given radius and color

getBoardSize.description: Returns the size of the board (the number of squares along each edge) for the current puzzle

describers.getBoardSize: retrieved the size of the board (the number of squares along each edge)
